description: Body for catalog-columns-create. Identified by (node_id, name).

clickhouse_type.description: Raw ClickHouse type string (`String`, `Nullable(DateTime64(3))`, `Array(String)`...). Set when the column comes from a ClickHouse-backed table; null for Postgres-only sources.

confidence.description: Agent confidence (0..1) in the description and semantic typing.

generator_model.description: Model that generated the description/typing — same convention as on nodes.

hogql_type.description: HogQL-normalized type — `String`, `Int`, `Float`, `Boolean`, `DateTime`, `Array`, `JSON`. What the agent sees when reading via `system.columns`. Inferred from clickhouse_type when not set explicitly.

name.description: Column name as it appears in the underlying table. Case-sensitive. Combined with `node_id` to form the upsert key — calling create again with the same (node_id, name) updates in place.

node_id.description: ID of the parent CatalogNode (returned by catalog-nodes-create).

nullable.description: Whether the column can hold NULL values. Drives null-handling guidance in generated queries.

pii_class.description:
Sensitivity classification. `pii` for personally identifiable (email, name, IP), `sensitive` for business-confidential, `public` for safe-to-export, `unknown` to defer classification.

* `pii` - pii
* `sensitive` - sensitive
* `public` - public
* `unknown` - unknown

position.description: Ordinal position of the column in the source table. Used for display and stable iteration.

anyOf.description:
* `entity_id` - entity_id
* `foreign_key` - foreign_key
* `timestamp` - timestamp
* `measure` - measure
* `dimension` - dimension
* `monetary` - monetary
* `free_text` - free_text
* `enum` - enum
* `uuid` - uuid
* `unknown` - unknown

semantic_type.description:
Role of the column for query planning. `entity_id` for primary identifiers, `foreign_key` for join targets, `timestamp` for time filtering, `measure` for aggregation, `dimension` for group-by, `monetary` for currency, `free_text` for unstructured prose, `enum` for closed value sets.

* `entity_id` - entity_id
* `foreign_key` - foreign_key
* `timestamp` - timestamp
* `measure` - measure
* `dimension` - dimension
* `monetary` - monetary
* `free_text` - free_text
* `enum` - enum
* `uuid` - uuid
* `unknown` - unknown

synthetic_description.description: What the column represents in business terms — meaning, units, valid values, gotchas. Example: "Subscription monthly recurring revenue in USD cents. Excludes refunds. Null for one-time charges."
